title: [podman/netavark] dns search domain not propagated to the container(s)
body:
### Issue Description

One a newly preparated machine, we're trying to reach a machine called "db" in the same domain as the host.
However, it seems that the dns search domain isn't propagated to the containers and therefore the resolution fails.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman & netavark
2. Ensure search is defined in /etc/resolv.conf
3. From the container, try to resolv a name without the domain name appended


### Describe the results you received

Inside the container, try to lookup for db.mydomain 
```
root@e89fa3c5cc65:/# < /dev/tcp/db/3306
bash: db: Name or service not known
bash: /dev/tcp/db/3306: Invalid argument
```
However, the dns information seems correct on container:
```
root@e89fa3c5cc65:/# cat /etc/resolv.conf 
search dns.podman
nameserver 10.89.0.1
```
And the hosts: 
```
[root@host~]# cat /etc/resolv.conf 
# Generated by NetworkManager
search mydomain
nameserver 10.0.72.1
``` 
Also the search domain is working on host "host"
```
[root@host~]# < /dev/tcp/db/3306
[root@host~]#
[root@host~]# < /dev/tcp/db.mydomain/3306
[root@host~]#
```


### Describe the results you expected

DNS search domain properly propagated to the container

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

RL8
netavark-1.10.3-1.module+el8.10.0+1948+4b5cd4a9.x86_64

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Upstream only support the latest release.

And if you look at the release notes for 5.5.1

> Fixed a bug where containers joined to a network with DNS enabled would not include the host's search domains in their resolv.conf (https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24713).

So this already works
